threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (1 messages, 2020-09-16) ---
[2020-09-16 16:56] mtanzer: the real issue with savior is the assumptions of the model just don't really apply to it.  for regular units it's fair to say the cost of the unit is pretty much only the buy price.  But if you want to compare buying savior to not buying savior, often buying it loses so much tempo for so many turns that you end up paying a much higher rate on soak to survive until it comes online.  also the model assumes units like plasmafier will attack every turn for a long time (or you could make some other specific assumption like they're all used as soak if you wanted) which just isn't true with savior.

also even if we tried to do a calculation for savior, the values would vary so much depending on the number of drones bought and on which turns, the number of plasmafiers made, on which turns the plasmafiers were clicked, and how many plasmafiers were used for soak.  you'd basically have to do a calculation for each game separately since the numbers would be different each time, and at that point you'd be much better off just playing around in analysis mode to compare lines buying savior to lines that don't.

--- Thread (4 messages, 2020-12-09) ---
[2020-12-09 01:36] velizar_ (Name): Two separate questions:
1. Do I want 3rd engi in a set with a lot of soak but wall absorb?
2. Do I want 3rd engi in a set with a good absorber, which is like Arka or Omega Splitter (with blue sink), but no soak? Note that those units can be clicked instead of absorbed on and you don't lose much
[2020-12-09 01:41] silentslayers: Someone will probably give a more precise answer, but *very* broadly speaking:

1. No
2. Yes
[2020-12-09 01:45] velizar_ (Name): I get that the standard advice is "get more economy if there's a good absorber"
[2020-12-09 01:46] velizar_ (Name): I'm interested in why cases 1 and 2 aren't exceptions to that rule.
1: If soak is cheap, then... inflation is low? inflation is strongest attacker vs best soak so actually it's more like "good soak, bad attacker"?

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content:
Two separate questions:
1. Do I want 3rd engi in a set with a lot of soak but wall absorb?
2. Do I want 3rd engi in a set with a good absorber, which is like Arka or Omega Splitter (with blue sink), but no soak? Note that those units can be clicked instead of absorbed on and you don't lose much

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content:
Someone will probably give a more precise answer, but *very* broadly speaking:

1. No
2. Yes

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content: I get that the standard advice is "get more economy if there's a good absorber"

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content:
I'm interested in why cases 1 and 2 aren't exceptions to that rule.
1: If soak is cheap, then... inflation is low? inflation is strongest attacker vs best soak so actually it's more like "good soak, bad attacker"?

messages.author: velizar_
messages.content:
2: Normally the first few attackers for you get no value while you're below absorb; however in case of e.g. doomed mech or mahar, when you build your 4 attackers and your opponent builds that absorber, then the existence of your last 2 attackers encourages your opponent to attack you for 2 less, so they are giving value for you; 
They are giving you less than full value, because they are allowing your opponent to threaten without attacking (thus making your opponent's absorber-attacker more valuable than if simply attacking).

[2020-12-09 03:07] jamberine: for 1 I think strong soak does slightly encourage high econ
[2020-12-09 03:08] jamberine: but the effect isn't that big
[2020-12-09 03:10] jamberine: a reason that comes to mind is that high quality soak tends to come in big chunks
[2020-12-09 03:10] jamberine: which makes it take a while to be relevant
[2020-12-09 03:11] jamberine: like your opponent needs 7 attack before you can aegis
[2020-12-09 03:12] jamberine: and also means you often can't benefit from more than one once you're over

[2020-12-09 03:25] jamberine: but you're down 6 defensive tempo

[2020-12-09 03:28] jamberine: so you have to buy 5 less defense

messages.content: you say you "don't lose much" for clicking omega splitter

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: I think you lose more than you would think

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: let's say you click omega splitter

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: now your opponent needs to buy 3 more defense next turn

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: but you're down 6 defensive tempo

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: so you buy two walls to defend

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: compare that to if you held it

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: instead of walls you buy infusion grids, since you're defended with the hold

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: now on your next turn, you got 3 more absorb, and two extra hp from buying infusions over walls

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: so you have to buy 5 less defense

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: this is much better than making your opponent buy 3 more defense half a turn earlier

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: infusion grid isn't always in the set of course, but infusion grid is "only" 4/3x better than wall for coming a turn later

messages.author: jamberine
messages.content: attackers can do better than that
